description: HTTP status code returned by the request layer before JSON parsing.
validation_rule: 200-299 expected for successful JSON capture; 429/401/403 are access diagnostics.
flattening_rule: Capture as request/response metadata
developer_notes: Required for distinguishing symbol-not-found from Yahoo rate limit/access failures.

description: Exact URL requested, after encoding query parameters.
validation_rule: Must not be blank in capture logs.
flattening_rule: Capture as request/response metadata
developer_notes: Store encoded final URL, not just template.

description: UTC timestamp when the request was sent.
validation_rule: Must be valid ISO-8601 UTC timestamp.
flattening_rule: Capture as request/response metadata
developer_notes: Needed because marketState and access behavior are time-dependent.

description: Symbol or query token requested by caller before Yahoo normalization.
flattening_rule: Capture as request/response metadata
developer_notes: Separate from returned symbol; useful when result is null or empty.

description: Normalized outcome of one endpoint request.
validation_rule: Must be one of result_state enumeration values.
flattening_rule: Capture as request/response metadata
developer_notes: Central routing field for parser and retry logic.

description: Count of returned result objects or top-level rows after JSON parse.
validation_rule: 0 with HTTP 200 is an empty result, not a transport failure.
flattening_rule: Capture as request/response metadata
developer_notes: Count quoteResponse.result[], chart.result[], search.quotes[], or finance.result[] depending endpoint.

description: HTTP Content-Type header seen by request layer.
validation_rule: Warn if not JSON for API capture.
flattening_rule: Capture as request/response metadata
developer_notes: Detect HTML/login/throttle pages before JSON parsing.

description: Short sanitized prefix of response body for diagnostics when parse fails or response is empty.
validation_rule: Limit to safe length; do not store cookies or secrets.
flattening_rule: Capture as request/response metadata
developer_notes: Useful for 429/HTML pages and malformed JSON.

description: Quote endpoint error object, if Yahoo returns one.
validation_rule: If error not null, set result_state=YAHOO_ERROR_OBJECT.
flattening_rule: Capture as request/response metadata
developer_notes: A null error with empty result is still an actionable capture condition.

description: Yahoo chart error code when chart endpoint rejects or cannot find a symbol/range.
validation_rule: Store text; do not hard-fail unknown codes.
flattening_rule: Capture as request/response metadata
developer_notes: Differentiate symbol-not-found from HTTP-layer failure.

description: Yahoo chart error description when present.
example_value: No data found, symbol may be delisted
flattening_rule: Capture as request/response metadata
developer_notes: Do not infer delisting from this field alone without quote/search cross-check.

description: Search endpoint count value from Yahoo response.
flattening_rule: Capture as request/response metadata
developer_notes: May not equal quotes array length when news/list modules are also included.

description: Derived count of quote results in search response.
validation_rule: 0 for q=AAPL should be captured as SEARCH_EMPTY_QUOTES and cross-checked with quote endpoint.
flattening_rule: Capture as request/response metadata
developer_notes: Search may be more reliable with company name fallback, e.g., q=Apple.

description: Screener endpoint error object, if Yahoo returns one.
validation_rule: If error not null, set result_state=YAHOO_ERROR_OBJECT.
flattening_rule: Capture as request/response metadata
developer_notes: Predefined screener has no AAPL symbol parameter; failures are endpoint/request/access issues.

description: Derived count of predefined screener result groups.
validation_rule: 0 with HTTP 200 should be captured as SCREENER_EMPTY_RESULT.
flattening_rule: Capture as request/response metadata
developer_notes: Do not classify as AAPL-specific because the predefined URL is not symbol-specific.

description: QuoteSummary endpoint error object, if Yahoo returns one.
validation_rule: If error not null, set result_state=YAHOO_ERROR_OBJECT.
flattening_rule: Capture as request/response metadata
developer_notes: QuoteSummary module failures can be module/access-specific; preserve requested module list.

description: Raw input text accepted by the capture harness before routing; may be a symbol, comma symbol list, query, screener id, or full URL.
validation_rule: Must be preserved exactly before normalization.
flattening_rule: Capture as request metadata; never pass full URL as a symbol.
developer_notes: Added after user logs showed full URLs treated as symbols by quoteSummary/Fundamentals routine.

description: Detected kind of input supplied to the app/router.
flattening_rule: Derive before making endpoint request or parser choice.
developer_notes: Use to prevent full URLs from entering symbol-only parsers.

description: Endpoint family determined from URL path or chosen symbol workflow.
validation_rule: Must match endpoint URL path and parser.
flattening_rule: Derive by URL path; for raw symbols derive from the selected operation.
developer_notes: Critical router field for v0.3.2.

description: Parser actually selected by the application for the response.
validation_rule: Should equal parser_expected except in diagnostic test cases.
flattening_rule: Capture at dispatch time before response parsing.
developer_notes: Logs showed Search/Screener/Chart URLs being processed as quote/fundamentals.

description: Parser that should handle the endpoint response based on endpoint_type.
validation_rule: Derived parser must be consistent with endpoint_type.
flattening_rule: Derive from endpoint_type by a table, not by URL text appearing in the symbol slot.
developer_notes: Router validation target.

description: Whether the selected parser matches the detected endpoint type.
validation_rule: False rows must not be interpreted as Yahoo data failure.
flattening_rule: Record as diagnostic and skip endpoint-specific financial extraction.
developer_notes: Key v0.3.2 guardrail.

description: Host name from URL input or generated request URL.
validation_rule: Required when raw input is a URL.
flattening_rule: Parse URL; blank for raw symbol inputs until request URL is generated.
developer_notes: Useful to distinguish query1/query2 and accidental non-Yahoo hosts.

description: URL path used to identify the endpoint family.
validation_rule: Must map to a known endpoint type or UNKNOWN_ENDPOINT_PATH.
flattening_rule: Parse URL and use routing table.
developer_notes: Prevents path-specific endpoint from being fed into quote parser.

description: URL query string from input or generated request URL.
validation_rule: Preserve raw query string for encoding triage.
flattening_rule: Capture as URL metadata before decoding.
developer_notes: Needed for special-symbol encoding checks.

description: Symbols requested by the URL or raw symbol-list input, after URL-decoding one time.
validation_rule: Preserve order and compare case-sensitively after canonicalization rules.
flattening_rule: Decode symbols parameter once; for chart/quoteSummary/options use path symbol.
developer_notes: Enables missing-symbol diagnostics for batched quote calls.

description: Symbols actually present in the endpoint response.
validation_rule: For quote use quoteResponse.result[].symbol; for chart use chart.result[].meta.symbol.
flattening_rule: Extract only from the endpoint-appropriate response shape.
developer_notes: The quote run returned MSFT first while AAPL was absent.

description: Requested symbols not found in the response result set.
validation_rule: Non-empty list should set result_state=REQUESTED_SYMBOL_MISSING_FROM_RESULT or PARTIAL_SYMBOL_RESULT.
flattening_rule: Compute after endpoint-specific parsing; do not infer invalid symbol without cross-check.
developer_notes: Primary v0.3.2 diagnostic for AAPL missing from batch quote result.

description: Symbols returned by Yahoo that were not explicitly requested.
flattening_rule: Compute after endpoint-specific parsing.
developer_notes: Placeholder for alias/canonical symbol normalization.

description: Count of requested symbols derived from input.
validation_rule: Must be >= returned_symbol_count only if no unexpected aliases are returned.
flattening_rule: Compute from requested_symbols list.
developer_notes: Used in run summary.

description: Count of returned symbols derived from parsed response.
validation_rule: If lower than requested count, compare missing_requested_symbols.
flattening_rule: Compute from returned_symbols list.
developer_notes: Used in run summary.

description: Routing error code, if route_valid is false or symbols are missing.
flattening_rule: Use first high-confidence routing error; keep additional details in diagnostic_summary.
developer_notes: Stops URL-as-symbol errors from being treated as Yahoo nulls.

description: Whether the original user/app input was a full URL.
validation_rule: TRUE implies router must parse endpoint instead of treating input as a symbol.
flattening_rule: Set before normalization.
developer_notes: Simple guardrail for app code.

description: Flag indicating a full URL reached a symbol-only parser such as quoteSummary/Fundamentals.
validation_rule: If TRUE, classify as app routing issue, not Yahoo symbol failure.
flattening_rule: Detect before calling quoteSummary or fundamentals routine.
developer_notes: Directly explains Not Found errors that name the full URL as the symbol.

description: Classification of URL encoding in the symbols/query parameters.
validation_rule: Special symbols missing after encoded request should trigger double-encoding check.
flattening_rule: Compare raw query string and decoded symbol list; detect %25 patterns.
developer_notes: Useful for futures/index/forex symbols containing = and ^.

description: Short human-readable classification of a capture anomaly.
example_value: AAPL requested but absent from quoteResponse.result[].symbol list.
validation_rule: Keep concise; avoid replacing structured fields.
flattening_rule: Build from result_state, route_error_code, counts, missing symbols, and endpoint_type.
developer_notes: Helps CSV review without opening raw JSON.
